summary: A doctest in `doc/callbacks.rst` failed during the documentation test phase. The doctest expected a progress bar output from `estimator.fit(X, y)` but instead got `LogisticRegression()` (the default repr), indicating the progress bar callback was not active or not registered on the estimator.
root_cause: The doctest in `doc/callbacks.rst` at line 48 expected the output `LogisticRegression - fit ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 100% 0:03:12` from calling `estimator.fit(X, y)`, but the actual output was `LogisticRegression()`. The progress bar callback was not registered on the estimator (the `set_callbacks` call earlier in the doctest was marked `# doctest: +SKIP`), so the fit call returned the plain estimator repr instead of the expected progress bar display.

evidence.reason: Pytest short test summary identifies the failing doctest as callbacks.rst
Pinpoints the exact file and line number of the doctest failure
Shows the mismatch: the doctest expected a progress bar output but got the plain estimator repr, because the set_callbacks call was skipped
The callback registration step was skipped, so the subsequent fit call had no callback registered and produced no progress bar output
Confirms exactly one test failed in the doc test run

suggested_fix: Either remove the `# doctest: +SKIP` from the `set_callbacks` call so the callback is actually registered before `fit` is called, or add `# doctest: +SKIP` to the `estimator.fit(X, y)` line as well (as the TODO comment in the doc suggests), so the expected-but-unachievable progress bar output is not tested.
